hypotheses.description: The parser call `return json.loads(Path(path).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))` at tools/evolution_bundle.py:548 has no nearby exception handling, so malformed input may escape as an opaque failure.
hypotheses.falsification_test: Feed malformed input to the function containing line 548; a named boundary error or explicit rejection falsifies the hypothesis.

hypotheses.description: The decision comparison `return False, f"Verdict {verdict} but speedup={speedup_s} < 1.0"` at tools/verify_bundle.py:404 uses a binary float threshold, so rounding at the boundary may flip the result.
hypotheses.falsification_test: Run inputs immediately below, exactly at, and above the threshold using exact decimal values; stable, documented boundary behavior falsifies the hypothesis.

limitations: Hypotheses require module 3 verification; parser candidates may receive isolated induction, while unsupported families remain AST-only.
